nodes.x: Moreover the Lord answered Job, and said,
Then Job answered the Lord, and said,
¶ Then Job answered the Lord, and said,
Then the Lord answered Job out of the whirlwind, and said,
¶ Then answered the Lord unto Job out of the whirlwind, and said,
And the Lord turned the captivity of Job, when he prayed for his friends: also the Lord gave Job twice as much as he had before.
Shall he that contendeth with the Almighty instruct him? he that reproveth God, let him answer it.

And he that will contend against the word of the Lord, let him be accursed; and he that shall deny these things, let him be accursed; for unto them will I show no greater things, saith Jesus Christ; for I am he who speaketh.

Yea, he that repenteth and exerciseth faith, and bringeth forth good works, and prayeth continually without ceasing—unto such it is given to know the mysteries of God; yea, unto such it shall be given to reveal things which never have been revealed; yea, and it shall be given unto such to bring thousands of souls to repentance, even as it has been given unto us to bring these our brethren to…
Will he delight himself in the Almighty? will he always call upon God?

As, however, he is in a critical cue, he will, perhaps, excuse our freedom in offering for his reflection and the exercise of his hyper-criticism a passage or two from the Bible, which he professes to venerate exclusively as “ the Word of God ” in reality. *We could, if necessary, furnish, him with many ; but one or two will suffice. He will find one in Gen. iii. 8,. which reads as follows:— “And…
Wo unto him that spurneth at the doings of the Lord; yea, wo unto him that shall deny the Christ and his works!
¶ Then Job answered the Lord, and said,
Moreover the Lord answered Job, and said,
Then Job answered and said,
Behold, I am vile; what shall I answer thee? I will lay mine hand upon my mouth.
And I will yet be more vile than thus, and will be base in mine own sight: and of the maidservants which thou hast spoken of, of them shall I be had in honour.
And I will cast abominable filth upon thee, and make thee vile, and will set thee as a gazingstock.
Behold, and lo, I am the Lord thy God, and will answer thee as touching this matter.
But if he thus say, I have no delight in thee; behold, here am I, let him do to me as seemeth good unto him.

Behold, therefore I have stretched out my hand over thee, and have diminished thine ordinary food, and delivered thee unto the will of them that hate thee, the daughters of the Philistines, which are ashamed of thy lewd way.
Once have I spoken; but I will not answer: yea, twice; but I will proceed no further.
I will speak, that I may be refreshed: I will open my lips and answer.
For God speaketh once, yea twice, yet man perceiveth it not.

"And I answered, ’It is even so, yet dare I not return and meet one who
has been deeply wronged by me in the past.’

I told you before, and foretell you, as if I were present, the second time; and being absent now I write to them which heretofore have sinned, and to all other, that, if I come again, I will not spare:
¶ Then answered the Lord unto Job out of the whirlwind, and said,
¶ Again the word of the Lord came unto me, saying,
Gird up thy loins now like a man: I will demand of thee, and declare thou unto me.
Gird up now thy loins like a man; for I will demand of thee, and answer thou me.
¶ Thou therefore gird up thy loins, and arise, and speak unto them all that I command thee: be not dismayed at their faces, lest I confound thee before them.
Hear, I beseech thee, and I will speak: I will demand of thee, and declare thou unto me.
Now, I say unto you, and what I say unto you, I say unto all the Twelve: Arise and gird up your loins, take up your cross, follow me, and feed my sheep.

And now, if thou wilt tell me concerning these things, whatsoever thou desirest I will give unto thee; and if it were needed, I would guard thee with my armies; but I know that thou art more powerful than all they; nevertheless, whatsoever thou desirest of me I will grant it unto thee.
Wilt thou also disannul my judgment? wilt thou condemn me, that thou mayest be righteous?

And now, my brethren, do ye suppose that God justifieth you in this thing? Behold, I say unto you, Nay. But he condemneth you, and if ye persist in these things his judgments must speedily come unto you.
I will say unto God, Do not condemn me; shew me wherefore thou contendest with me.
And dost thou open thine eyes upon such an one, and bringest me into judgment with thee?
Against thee, thee only, have I sinned, and done this evil in thy sight: that thou mightest be justified when thou speakest, and be clear when thou judgest.
Therefore the ungodly shall not stand in the judgment, nor sinners in the congregation of the righteous.
Hast thou an arm like God? or canst thou thunder with a voice like him?
Thou hast a mighty arm: strong is thy hand, and high is thy right hand.
God thundereth marvellously with his voice; great things doeth he, which we cannot comprehend.
Hast thou given the horse strength? hast thou clothed his neck with thunder?
O Lord, there is none like thee, neither is there any God beside thee, according to all that we have heard with our ears.
Deck thyself now with majesty and excellency; and array thyself with glory and beauty.
Honour and majesty are before him: strength and beauty are in his sanctuary.
I will speak of the glorious honour of thy majesty, and of thy wondrous works.
¶ O thou afflicted, tossed with tempest, and not comforted, behold, I will lay thy stones with fair colours, and lay thy foundations with sapphires.
But beauty and glory are over thee blended, Shed forth by Jehovah in majesty bright.'
Cast abroad the rage of thy wrath: and behold every one that is proud, and abase him.
Proud and haughty scorner is his name, who dealeth in proud wrath.
He who smote the people in wrath with a continual stroke, he that ruled the nations in anger, is persecuted, and none hindereth.
Declare his glory among the heathen; his marvellous works among all nations.
Wrath is cruel, and anger is outrageous; but who is able to stand before envy?
Look on every one that is proud, and bring him low; and tread down the wicked in their place.
Admonish him in his faults, and also receive admonition of him. Be patient; be sober; be temperate; have patience, faith, hope and charity.
Sing unto him, sing psalms unto him, talk ye of all his wondrous works.
Make you perfect in every good work to do his will, working in you that which is wellpleasing in his sight, through Jesus Christ; to whom be glory for ever and ever. Amen.
Continue in the spirit of meekness, and beware of pride. Let thy soul delight in thy husband, and the glory which shall come upon him.
Though the Lord be high, yet hath he respect unto the lowly: but the proud he knoweth afar off.
Hide them in the dust together; and bind their faces in secret.
Let their eyes be darkened, that they see not; and make their loins continually to shake.
Let them not depart from thine eyes; keep them in the midst of thine heart.
Let their eyes be darkened, that they may not see, and bow down their back alway.
And join them one to another into one stick; and they shall become one in thine hand.
Bind them continually upon thine heart, and tie them about thy neck.
Then will I also confess unto thee that thine own right hand can save thee.
For I the Lord thy God will hold thy right hand, saying unto thee, Fear not; I will help thee.
Also I say unto you, Whosoever shall confess me before men, him shall the Son of man also confess before the angels of God:
Whosoever therefore shall confess me before men, him will I confess also before my Father which is in heaven.
And the righteous shall be gathered on my right hand unto eternal life; and the wicked on my left hand will I be ashamed to own before the Father;
And now, behold, we are in thine hand: as it seemeth good and right unto thee to do unto us, do.
¶ Behold now behemoth, which I made with thee; he eateth grass as an ox.
Thus they changed their glory into the similitude of an ox that eateth grass.

And they shall drive thee from men, and thy dwelling shall be with the beasts of the field: they shall make thee to eat grass as oxen, and seven times shall pass over thee, until thou know that the most High ruleth in the kingdom of men, and giveth it to whomsoever he will.

¶ And thou, son of man, prophesy and say, Thus saith the Lord God concerning the Ammonites, and concerning their reproach; even say thou, The sword, the sword is drawn: for the slaughter it is furbished, to consume because of the glittering:

And he was driven from the sons of men; and his heart was made like the beasts, and his dwelling was with the wild asses: they fed him with grass like oxen, and his body was wet with the dew of heaven; till he knew that the most high God ruled in the kingdom of men, and that he appointeth over it whomsoever he will.

And again, he saith thou shalt be as the blossoms of a thistle, which, when it is fully ripe, if the wind bloweth, it is driven forth upon the face of the land. And he pretendeth the Lord hath spoken it. And he saith all this shall come upon thee except thou repent, and this because of thine iniquities.
Lo now, his strength is in his loins, and his force is in the navel of his belly.
He giveth power to the faint; and to them that have no might he increaseth strength.
His strength shall be hungerbitten, and destruction shall be ready at his side.
She girdeth her loins with strength, and strengtheneth her arms.
In his hand are the deep places of the earth: the strength of the hills is his also.
And he took him by the right hand, and lifted him up: and immediately his feet and ankle bones received strength.
He moveth his tail like a cedar: the sinews of his stones are wrapped together.
His roots are wrapped about the heap, and seeth the place of stones.
The tabernacle, his tent, and his covering, his taches, and his boards, his bars, his pillars, and his sockets,
The flakes of his flesh are joined together: they are firm in themselves; they cannot be moved.
Sharp stones are under him: he spreadeth sharp pointed things upon the mire.
He heweth him down cedars, and taketh the cypress and the oak, which he strengtheneth for himself among the trees of the forest: he planteth an ash, and the rain doth nourish it.
His bones are as strong pieces of brass; his bones are like bars of iron.
He esteemeth iron as straw, and brass as rotten wood.
His legs of iron, his feet part of iron and part of clay.
And he had greaves of brass upon his legs, and a target of brass between his shoulders.
He keepeth all his bones: not one of them is broken.
Is my strength the strength of stones? or is my flesh of brass?
He is the chief of the ways of God: he that made him can make his sword to approach unto him.

And out of his mouth goeth a sharp sword, that with it he should smite the nations: and he shall rule them with a rod of iron: and he treadeth the winepress of the fierceness and wrath of Almighty God.
And the sword shall abide on his cities, and shall consume his branches, and devour them, because of their own counsels.
He gave his people over also unto the sword; and was wroth with his inheritance.

For he is the minister of God to thee for good. But if thou do that which is evil, be afraid; for he beareth not the sword in vain: for he is the minister of God, a revenger to execute wrath upon him that doeth evil.
When they which were about him saw what would follow, they said unto him, Lord, shall we smite with the sword?
Surely the mountains bring him forth food, where all the beasts of the field play.
The range of the mountains is his pasture, and he searcheth after every green thing.
And there was there an herd of many swine feeding on the mountain: and they besought him that he would suffer them to enter into them. And he suffered them.
For, behold, the beasts of the field and the fowls of the air, and that which cometh of the earth, is ordained for the use of man for food and for raiment, and that he might have in abundance.
He causeth the grass to grow for the cattle, and herb for the service of man: that he may bring forth food out of the earth;
He lieth under the shady trees, in the covert of the reed, and fens.
He sacrificed also and burnt incense in the high places, and on the hills, and under every green tree.
The shady trees cover him with their shadow; the willows of the brook compass him about.
And he sacrificed and burnt incense in the high places, and on the hills, and under every green tree.
And he spake of trees, from the cedar tree that is in Lebanon even unto the hyssop that springeth out of the wall: he spake also of beasts, and of fowl, and of creeping things, and of fishes.
The shady trees cover him with their shadow; the willows of the brook compass him about.
He took also of the seed of the land, and planted it in a fruitful field; he placed it by great waters, and set it as a willow tree.
He lieth under the shady trees, in the covert of the reed, and fens.
He is green before the sun, and his branch shooteth forth in his garden.
And the ravens brought him bread and flesh in the morning, and bread and flesh in the evening; and he drank of the brook.

τ: 1. He showed me a great willow, covering plains rhe 
and mountains, and under the cover of the willow- P2730! 
tree all had come who were called by the name of Wow-tree 
the Lord. 2. And there stood an angel of the Lord, 
glorious and very tall, by the side of the willow, with
Behold, he drinketh up a river, and hasteth not: he trusteth that he can draw up Jordan into his mouth.
And will go all of you armed over Jordan before the Lord, until he hath driven out his enemies from before him,

3. Now, therefore, as Gideon was relating this to some young men, they
believed him, and immediately there was an army of ten thousand men got
ready for fighting. But God stood by Gideon in his sleep, and told him
that mankind were too fond of themselves, and were enemies to such as
excelled in virtue. Now that they might not pass God over, but ascribe
the victory to him, and might not fancy it…

And it shall come to pass, as soon as the soles of the feet of the priests that bear the ark of the Lord, the Lord of all the earth, shall rest in the waters of Jordan, that the waters of Jordan shall be cut off from the waters that come down from above; and they shall stand upon an heap.
So he went and did according unto the word of the Lord: for he went and dwelt by the brook Cherith, that is before Jordan.
He taketh it with his eyes: his nose pierceth through snares.
Canst thou put an hook into his nose? or bore his jaw through with a thorn?
The snare is laid for him in the ground, and a trap for him in the way.
For he is cast into a net by his own feet, and he walketh upon a snare.
Canst thou fill his skin with barbed irons? or his head with fish spears?
He moveth his tail like a cedar: the sinews of his stones are wrapped together.
